match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"subagent_type":"research","task_description":"Gather detailed intel on LAX (Los Angeles International Airport) and JFK (John F. Kennedy International Airport). For each airport, include: major terminals and which airlines use them, typical security/TSA wait times (general ranges), common ground-transportation options and approximate durations to downtown (taxi/rideshare/public transit), typical weather patterns by season, any usual advisories or construction issues (if known), recommendations on how early to arrive for domestic and international flights, and any traveler tips (lounges, expedited security, parking). Assume no travel dates provided."}

match.userMessage: Gather detailed intel on LAX (Los Angeles International Airport) and JFK (John F. Kennedy International Airport). For each airport, include: major terminals and which airlines use them, typical security/TSA wait times (general ranges), common ground-transportation options and approximate durations to downtown (taxi/rideshare/public transit), typical weather patterns by season, any usual advisories or construction issues (if known), recommendations on how early to arrive for domestic and international flights, and any traveler tips (lounges, expedited security, parking). Assume no travel dates provided.

response.content:
Summary (tool check): LAX — Los Angeles International (USA) has 9 terminals and 4 runways; current reported conditions: 72°F, partly cloudy. JFK — John F. Kennedy International (USA) has 6 terminals and 4 runways; current reported conditions: 58°F, clear. Below are detailed, traveler-focused intel for each airport (no travel date assumed).

LAX (Los Angeles International)
- Major terminals / common airline locations
  - Terminal 1: Southwest (main base for Southwest). Terminal 2–3: Delta and some partners operate from these central terminals. Terminals 4–5: American Airlines and partners. Terminal 6: Alaska and some domestic carriers. Terminals 7–8: United. Tom Bradley International / Terminal B (TBIT): most long-haul international carriers (British Airways, Qantas, Air France, Lufthansa, many others) and many partner/international arrivals. Airline assignments can change — always verify with your carrier.
- Typical security / TSA wait times
  - TSA PreCheck: often 5–15 minutes. General screening: off-peak 5–25 minutes; weekday morning and evening peaks commonly 20–45 minutes; holiday/peak periods can see 45–90+ minutes (especially at TBIT for big international departures).
- Ground transportation & approximate times to Downtown LA (DTLA/Union Station)
  - Taxi / rideshare: typically 25–45 minutes off-peak; 45–90+ minutes in heavy traffic (rush hours/holidays).  
  - FlyAway bus (direct to Union Station): ~30–45 minutes depending on traffic — a reliable public option.  
  - Metro (shuttle to LAX/airport station + rail): 45–75+ minutes to central Manhattan-equivalent downtown depending on connections (cheaper but slower).
- Typical weather patterns by season
  - Winter (Dec–Feb): mild, occasional rain; daytime mid‑50s–mid‑60s°F.  
  - Spring (Mar–May): mild and warming, 60s–70s°F.  
  - Summer (Jun–Aug): warm to hot, 70s–80s+°F; coastal marine layer (“June/Gloom”) can produce cool, cloudy mornings.  
  - Fall (Sep–Nov): warm and dry early fall, cooling into mild conditions later.
- Advisories / construction notes
  - LAX has had multi-year modernization projects (terminal renovations, landside access improvements, automated people-mover and rental car center work) that have periodically affected curbside access, shuttle/rideshare pickup locations, and roadway traffic. Check the LAX website or your airline for current terminal/curbside/pickup advisories the week of travel.
- Recommendations on arrival time
  - Domestic: plan on 2 hours before departure.  
  - International: plan on 3 hours (more if you need to check bags or expect long immigration lines on arrival).
- Traveler tips
  - Lounges: many carriers operate lounges in TBIT and major terminals (Delta Sky Club, United Club, American Admirals Clubs, partner lounges). Priority Pass access available at select lounges/locations.  
  - Expedited security: TSA PreCheck is available and very helpful; CLEAR is available at LAX and speeds identity checks where offered.  
  - Parking: on-site terminal parking is convenient but expensive; long-term/economy lots or off-site parking shuttles are cheaper. Reserve parking ahead on busy days.  
  - Pickup/dropoff: familiarize yourself with current rideshare/taxi pickup locations (these have changed with construction/APM); allow extra time for curbside dropoffs and rideshare waits.

JFK (John F. Kennedy International)
- Major terminals / common airline locations
  - Terminal 1: many international carriers (varies by alliance and season).  
  - Terminal 2: smaller; some international and domestic services.  
  - Terminal 4: major international hub used by Delta and many international carriers and alliances (large CBP/immigration facility).  
  - Terminal 5: JetBlue’s primary base (large JetBlue presence).  
  - Terminal 7: British Airways and other carriers.  
  - Terminal 8: American Airlines hub operations. Airline/terminal usage shifts periodically — always confirm with your carrier.
- Typical security / TSA wait times
  - TSA PreCheck: often 5–15 minutes at many terminals. General screening: off-peak 5–25 minutes; morning/evening peaks often 20–45 minutes; holiday/seasonal peaks or heavy international days can push waits 45–90+ minutes. U.S. CBP immigration on arrival can be lengthy (commonly 30–90 minutes during busy periods).
- Ground transportation & approximate times to Manhattan (midtown/Penn Station)
  - Taxi / rideshare: typical travel time 30–60+ minutes depending on traffic (can be much longer at peak times).  
  - AirTrain + LIRR (via Jamaica): AirTrain + LIRR to Penn Station is commonly ~30–45 minutes total (fastest public option to Midtown).  
  - AirTrain + subway (A or E lines depending on station): ~45–75+ minutes to Midtown depending on connections. The AirTrain ride itself is ~10–15 minutes between terminals and Jamaica/Howard Beach.
- Typical weather patterns by season
  - Winter (Dec–Feb): cold, possible snow/ice; temps often 20s–40s°F and winter weather can cause delays.  
  - Spring (Mar–May): variable cool-to-mild, 40s–60s°F.  
  - Summer (Jun–Aug): warm to hot and humid, 70s–90s°F; thunderstorms possible.  
  - Fall (Sep–Nov): mild and comfortable, cooling through fall.
- Advisories / construction notes
  - JFK has been undergoing phased redevelopments (multi‑terminal projects, upgrades to Terminal 1 and other terminals) and periodic construction that can affect road access and terminal facilities. Terminal 4 is a frequent entry point for many international flights and can have long CBP processing times during peaks — check Port Authority / airline advisories.
- Recommendations on arrival time
  - Domestic: plan on 2 hours before departure.  
  - International: plan on 3 hours before departure; increase to 3.5–4 hours if traveling during peak holiday periods or if you need to check bags or require special assistance.
- Traveler tips
  - Lounges: strong lounge presence in T4, T5 (JetBlue/T5-operated lounges), T7/T8 (British Airways/AA/partners). Many international carriers provide partner lounges at their respective terminals.  
  - Expedited security: TSA PreCheck is widely available and recommended; CLEAR is available at some terminals and can shorten identity verification. Global Entry is very helpful for arrival immigration lines into the U.S.  
  - Parking / access: on‑airport parking is convenient but costly; consider drop-off, off-site parking, or car services for cost savings. Use the AirTrain for transfers between terminals and to access rail connections.
  - Arrival immigration: if arriving internationally, prepare for passport control and customs time—have documents ready and fill out any required forms/apps ahead of arrival.

If you’d like, I can:
- Check the current terminal/airline assignment for a specific flight number, or
- Pull the latest official airport advisories/construction notices for LAX and JFK for travel-week updates. Which would you prefer?

toolCalls.arguments: {"subagent_type":"booking","task_description":"Find flight options between LAX (Los Angeles) and JFK (New York). No travel dates provided — provide general, traveler-focused information: major nonstop carriers and typical number of daily nonstop flights; typical nonstop flight duration; common one-stop carriers and typical layover cities; typical departure terminals at LAX and arrival terminals at JFK for major carriers; typical fare ranges (one-way and roundtrip) by cabin class (economy/basic economy, main cabin, premium economy, business) for bookings made ~1–3 months in advance and for last-minute bookings (within 2 weeks); baggage fee norms for main US carriers; frequent flier/tier recommendations; recommended booking window and tips for finding lower fares. Also note any typical peak times of day and advice on nonstop vs. one-stop tradeoffs. At the end, ask the user if they have travel dates, preferred times, or seat class so you can find exact flights."}

Find flight options between LAX (Los Angeles) and JFK (New York). No travel dates provided — provide general, traveler-focused information: major nonstop carriers and typical number of daily nonstop flights; typical nonstop flight duration; common one-stop carriers and typical layover cities; typical departure terminals at LAX and arrival terminals at JFK for major carriers; typical fare ranges (one-way and roundtrip) by cabin class (economy/basic economy, main cabin, premium economy, business) for bookings made ~1–3 months in advance and for last-minute bookings (within 2 weeks); baggage fee norms for main US carriers; frequent flier/tier recommendations; recommended booking window and tips for finding lower fares. Also note any typical peak times of day and advice on nonstop vs. one-stop tradeoffs. At the end, ask the user if they have travel dates, preferred times, or seat class so you can find exact flights.

response.content:
Thanks — I pulled the current route info and below is a concise summary of a real available nonstop I found plus traveler-focused guidance for LAX ↔ JFK (no dates provided).

Quick sample flight options (real + representative)
- UA 123 — United (real result): departs LAX 08:00 → arrives JFK 16:30, duration ~5h30 (330 min), aircraft: Boeing 787. (Status: on time in dataset.)
- Representative — American Airlines (typical nonstop): departs early morning or late afternoon, aircraft commonly A321neo or A321 (duration ~5h30–6h10). 
- Representative — JetBlue (typical nonstop): multiple daily, including main-cabin A321 transcon and premium Mint product (if available); duration ~5h30–6h15.

If you want, I can search exact flights for particular dates/times and return 3–5 live-priced options.

Route-level facts travelers care about
- Major nonstop carriers: JetBlue, American (AA), Delta (DL), United (UA). These four operate the bulk of nonstop LAX–JFK service.
- Typical number of daily nonstop flights: roughly 20–35 nonstop departures per day combined (varies by day/season; busiest in summer and holidays).
- Typical nonstop flight duration: about 5 hr 30 min to 6 hr 30 min (winds and routing cause variations).
- Common one‑stop carriers & layover cities: most airlines offer one-stop options via their hubs — AA via DFW or CLT or PHX; Delta via ATL, MSP or DTW; United via ORD, DEN or IAH; other options via BOS, SFO depending on carrier.
- Typical LAX departure terminals (major carriers):
  - American: Terminal 4/5 (A/B gates area)
  - Delta: Terminal 2 (and sometimes Terminal 3/5 for some flights/partners)
  - JetBlue: Terminal 5
  - United: Terminal 7 (United controls T7/T8 complex)
  (Always confirm day-of — LAX gate/terminal assignments can change.)
- Typical JFK arrival terminals (major carriers):
  - American: Terminal 8
  - Delta: Terminal 4
  - JetBlue: Terminal 5
  - United: Terminal 7
  (At JFK also double-check — some codeshares or international connecting flights may use other terminals.)

Typical fares (approximate ranges; economy = main cabin unless noted)
- Booked ~1–3 months in advance
  - Basic economy (one‑way): $90–$180; roundtrip: $180–$350
  - Main cabin / economy (one‑way): $120–$260; roundtrip: $240–$450
  - Premium economy (where offered) (one‑way): $300–$550; roundtrip: $600–$1,100
  - Business / domestic premium (one‑way): $900–$2,000; roundtrip: $1,800–$4,000
- Last‑minute (within 2 weeks)
  - Basic economy (one‑way): $150–$400; roundtrip: $300–$800
  - Main cabin (one‑way): $200–$500; roundtrip: $400–$1,000
  - Premium economy (one‑way): $450–$900; roundtrip: $900–$1,800
  - Business (one‑way): $1,500–$4,500; roundtrip: $3,000–$8,000
Notes: ranges vary by season, weekday vs weekend, holidays, and promotional sales. JetBlue Mint and transcon premium seats often command a premium but can be good value on this route.

Baggage & carry-on norms (main U.S. legacy carriers)
- Carry-on: most standard economy tickets include a full-size carry-on + personal item; Basic Economy restrictions vary by airline (some carriers limit carry-on to a personal item only — check the airline’s basic-economy policy).
- Checked bags: typical fees on main carriers — $30 for 1st checked bag, $40–$45 for 2nd; premium cabins and many status levels include free checked bags.
- Oversize/overweight fees apply per airline — check airline’s fee chart before travel.

Frequent‑flyer / tier recommendations
- If you fly LAX–JFK frequently, pick one carrier/alliance and concentrate travel to build status (AA / OneWorld, DL / SkyTeam, UA / Star Alliance, or JetBlue Mosaic).
- Priority benefits to seek: free checked bags, priority boarding/security, lounge access (for long transcon flights, lounge access is valuable), complimentary upgrades for elites.
- For occasional travelers, a co‑brand airline credit card that covers first checked bag and priority boarding (and offers award miles) is often the most useful perk.

Booking window & money‑saving tips
- Recommended booking window: 3–8 weeks before travel for best balance of price/availability on this busy domestic transcon route; 1–3 months ahead often yields the best choice of times/seats for typical travelers.
- Tips for lower fares:
  - Be flexible with dates and airports (also check LGA and EWR if NYC is fine).
  - Use fare alerts (Google Flights, Hopper, Skyscanner) and monitor price drops.
  - Compare nonstop vs 1‑stop: one‑stops can save money but add travel time and risk of missed connections.
  - Off-peak times (late-night red-eyes or mid-day off-peak) sometimes cheaper.
  - Consider airline sales and quickly-bookable award inventory if you have points.
  - For Mint/premium seats, check seat-only upgrades and day‑of upgrade offers.

Peak travel times & nonstop vs one‑stop tradeoffs
- Peak times: morning (roughly 06:30–10:00) and evening (16:30–20:30) are busiest for nonstop departures; mid-day and red-eye (overnight) options exist but schedules vary.
- Nonstop advantages: much faster, simpler (no connection risk), often worth a modest premium.
- One‑stop advantages: can be significantly cheaper, more schedule options, sometimes better premium-cabin availability. Downsides: longer total trip time and greater chance of missed connections/delays.

Anything else I should know?
- If you want lowest risk and fastest travel, choose a nonstop in the cabin you prefer.
- If you prioritize price over time, compare 1-stop options (watch total connection time).
- For premium comfort consider JetBlue Mint (if offered on the date) or domestic transcon business on AA/DL/UA.

Would you like me to search exact flights and live fares? If so, tell me: travel dates (or date range), preferred departure time(s), cabin class, and whether you prefer nonstop only — I’ll pull the best 3 options with times, aircraft, and current price estimates.

match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"subagent_type":"itinerary","task_description":"Synthesize a concise final trip plan combining the research summary for LAX and JFK and the booking guidance for LAX→JFK. Produce a 1-2 sentence summary recommendation tailored to a typical traveler flying economy nonstop who values speed and reliability. Include recommended arrival time at LAX, suggested fare class, and quick tips for luggage and ground transport at both airports."}

match.userMessage: Synthesize a concise final trip plan combining the research summary for LAX and JFK and the booking guidance for LAX→JFK. Produce a 1-2 sentence summary recommendation tailored to a typical traveler flying economy nonstop who values speed and reliability. Include recommended arrival time at LAX, suggested fare class, and quick tips for luggage and ground transport at both airports.

response.content:
Take a nonstop morning LAX→JFK on a reliable carrier to maximize on-time performance and avoid connections; morning departures shorten the chance of cascading delays. Expect mild, sunny conditions leaving LAX and more variable weather at JFK (summer thunderstorms or winter snow can cause delays), so keep an eye on forecasts the day before travel. Arrive at LAX about 2 hours before departure (2.5–3 hours if you need to check bags or travel during peak periods), check your airline terminal in advance, and use TSA PreCheck/CLEAR if available to speed security. Pack a single carry‑on to skip checked‑baggage waits; at LAX use the terminal rideshare/LAX-it pickup or prebook a car, and at JFK favor AirTrain + LIRR/subway for Manhattan or a rideshare/taxi from the terminal pickup zone.

Recommendation: For a traveler who values speed and reliability, book Main Cabin Extra (or add priority boarding) on a nonstop morning flight, arrive 2 hours early at LAX, travel with carry‑on only if possible, and use rideshare at LAX and AirTrain+LIRR or a terminal rideshare at JFK for fastest ground transfer.

match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

response.content:
Plan ready: I’ve gathered airport intel for LAX and JFK, summarized typical flight options and fares for LAX→JFK, and prepared a concise trip recommendation.

Final 1–2 sentence recommendation:
Take a nonstop morning flight from LAX to JFK in Main Cabin (or Main Cabin Extra for extra legroom/priority boarding) to maximize on-time reliability and minimize connection risk. Arrive at LAX about 2 hours before departure (2.5–3 hours if you need to check bags), travel with a carry-on if possible, and use rideshare pickup at LAX and AirTrain + LIRR or terminal rideshare/taxi at JFK for fastest transfers.

If you’d like exact flights and live fares, tell me your travel dates (or date range), preferred departure times, and cabin class (nonstop only?), and I’ll pull 3 recommended options.
